reasoning:
We have 67 days (approx. 0.18 years) remaining until the July 1, 2025 resolution date. From 2020 through 2023, Africa experienced a dense cluster of successful coups (averaging ~2 per year, e.g., in Mali, Burkina Faso, Niger, Gabon). However, the trend abruptly halted—there were exactly zero successful coups recorded globally in 2024 and none in the first four months of 2025, implying that the 'coup contagion' wave has largely dissipated or that surviving regimes have successfully coup-proofed. If we adjust the baseline expected rate to roughly 1 successful coup per year, the mathematical probability of at least one coup occurring over a 0.18-year period is approximately 16%. 

I evaluated potential edge cases, particularly regarding the resolution criterion that includes 'scenarios in which an incumbent leader refuses to relinquish power after an election' (a self-coup). This explicitly maps to the widely disputed July 2024 Venezuelan election and Nicolas Maduro's subsequent January 2025 inauguration. However, because his stay in power was ratified by Venezuela's highly integrated state/electoral institutions, mainstream reporting and political scientists overwhelmingly categorize the event as 'electoral fraud' and a continuation of an autocracy, rather than a new 'coup d'état'. The probability of the question resolving YES on this technicality alone is very low (<3%). 

Recent prominent events, such as the June 2024 mobilization in Bolivia and the January 2025 militant attacks in Chad, were unsuccessful and rapidly suppressed. With no ongoing mutinies or visibly imminent military overthrows poised to succeed in the coming two months, my forecast relies heavily on the time-adjusted base rate of ~16% plus a minimal premium for edge cases. I estimate the probability at 17%.
